Bio CV Students Teaching Awards Publications

Ravi Netravali

Associate Professor of Computer Science, Princeton University
rnetravali at cs dot princeton dot edu
35 Olden Street, Office 322; Princeton, NJ 08544

Co-Lead, Princeton Systems for AI Lab (SAIL)
Co-Founder, BreezeML

I am an Associate Professor in the Computer Science Department at Princeton University. My research interests are primarily in AI systems, with a recent focus on improving the performance, accuracy, and resource efficiency of current and emerging generative workloads across phases (training, post-training, inference), scales (cloud and edge), and modalities (text, image, video). My research has been recognized with a Sloan Research Fellowship, the SIGCOMM Rising Star Award, an NSF CAREER Award, multiple industry faculty research awards, and an IRTF Applied Networking Research Prize. Prior to joining Princeton, I was an Assistant Professor at UCLA from 2019-2021. Even earlier, I received a Ph.D. from MIT in 2018, and a B.S. in Electrical Engineering from Columbia University in 2012.

Students

If you are interested in working with me, please apply to the Princeton CS graduate program, and mention my name in your application. If you are already at Princeton, please send me an email and we can find a time to meet.

  • Mihir Mathur (Masters 2020, co-advised with Miryung Kim), first employment: Lyft

Teaching

COS 597a (Princeton): Systems for Serving Generative AI (Fall 2024)

COS 316 (Princeton): Principles of Computer System Design (Fall 2022)

COS 598a (Princeton): ML-driven Video Systems (Spring 2022, Fall 2020, Winter 2020)

COS 561 (Princeton): Advanced Computer Networks (Fall 2021, Spring 2023)

CS 219 (UCLA): Web and Mobile Systems (Fall 2019, Winter 2019)

CS 134 (UCLA): Distributed Systems (Spring 2020, Spring 2019)

2025 MLSys Outstanding Paper Award, Honorable Mention

2023 Howard B. Wentz Junior Faculty Award (Princeton University)

2021 Facebook Faculty Award

2020 NSF CAREER Award

2019 ACM SoCC Best Paper Award
